'''David Maiden''' (born 7 August 1971) is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer and coach played in the 1990s and 2000s. A {{rlp|lk}} or {{rlp|ce}}, he played for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], [[Gateshead Thunder (1999)|Gateshead Thunder]] and [[Hull FC]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/david-maiden/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref>

==Playing career==
A [[Ravenshoe, Queensland|Ravenshoe]] junior, Maiden represented the [[Queensland Residents rugby league team|Queensland Residents]] side in 1994, playing in their game against the New South Wales Residents and their tour of South Africa.<ref>{{cite book |last=Cadigan|first=Neil|title=25 Years in the Saddle|year=2015|publisher=Playright Publishing|page=81|isbn=9780994237316}}</ref>

In 1995, Maiden joined the newly established [[North Queensland Cowboys]]. In Round 2 of the [[1995 ARL season]], he made his first grade debut in a 16–34 loss to the [[Sydney City Roosters]], filling in at centre for the suspended [[Adrian Vowles]]. Maiden played just two games for the Cowboys before leaving at the end of the season.

From 1996 to 1998, Maiden played for the [[Cairns Cyclones]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].

In 1999, Maiden moved to England, joining the [[Gateshead Thunder (1999)|Gateshead Thunder]] in the [[Super League]]. He played 24 games for Gateshead that season, scoring eight tries. In October 1999, he represented [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] in the Triangular Series.

In 2000, Maiden joined [[Hull F.C.]] after the club merged with Gateshead. Later that year, he represented Scotland at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]], scoring a try against [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]].<ref name="The Teams: Scotland">{{cite web|url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/hi/english/static/in_depth/rugby_league/2000/world_cup/team_profiles/scotland.stm|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121108193924/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/hi/english/static/in_depth/rugby_league/2000/world_cup/team_profiles/scotland.stm|url-status=dead|archive-date=8 November 2012|title=The Teams: Scotland (archived)|publisher=BBC Sport|date=31 December 2009|access-date=13 January 2010}}</ref> In July 2001, he again represented Scotland in a 42–20 win over [[France national rugby league team|France]], scoring a try. At the end of the 2001 season, Maiden left Hull after playing 49 games for the club.

==Coaching career==
In 2008, Maiden became an assistant coach of the [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]] for their [[2008 Northern Pride RLFC season|inaugural season]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]. In 2010, he took over as head coach of the club. In his [[2010 Northern Pride RLFC season|first season in charge]], he led the club to their first premiership after defeating the [[Norths Devils]] in the Grand Final.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2019/09/25/flashback-sheppard-inspires-2010-maiden-title-for-pride/|title=Flashback: Sheppard inspires 2010 maiden title for Pride|date=25 September 2019|website=qrl.com.au}}</ref> He left the club at the end of the [[2012 Northern Pride RLFC season|2012 season]].

==Post-playing career==
Since 2015, Maiden has worked as the [[Queensland Rugby League]]'s major competitions manager.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/20182/09/06/competitions-manager-faces-the-grill/|title=Competitions Manager faces the grill|date=6 September 2019|website=qrl.com.au}}</ref>
